title: Rich Velvety Dutch Apple Pudding
description: A sour and velvety Dutch dessert featuring marsala wine and long fermentation dough, perfect for midnight cravings. This shojin-friendly pudding is a unique treat for those looking for something new.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped apples, marsala wine, and sugar, and let it sit for 30 minutes to allow the apples to soften.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the long fermentation dough, eggs, flour, and melted butter until a smooth batter forms.
Add the cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt to the batter and mix well.
Pour the batter over the apple mixture and stir until just combined.
Pour the mixture into a 9x13 inch baking dish and bake for 40 minutes.
Remove the pudding from the oven and let it cool to room temperature.
Cover the pudding with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow it to chill and set.
Serve the pudding chilled, garnished with a sprinkle of cinnamon if desired.
